author: jsign
content:
I wanted to do a temp check reg a proposal for editing `ChainConfig` to remove the [blob_schedule field](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/f03c2e0af2df95cd2eed029ba4ea7140acd028c7/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/stateless.py#L173). 

The motivation was raised by Han working on Zkboost and realizing that CLs do not have the target blobs or update fraction in their config (these values are only useful for EL to update fees). This creates a challenge for the CL in checking public inputs since doesn’t have that information in its own chain config.

The proposal could be to remove this `BlobSchedule` field, and expect the guest program to have a kind of map like `map[chain_id][Fork][BlobSchedule]`, so at least we keep guest programs generic by `chain_id`. (Which was the original intention to potentially help supporting native rollups with the same guest program or other devnets).

cc <@466102668138512394> <@912415191067676693> <@126385519469789184> <@281967554572976128> <@976542403269910539>  <@402080271563751435>  to get your thoughts.

author: 0xrubo
content: i give my support. the simpler, the better. at the moment, we override our blob schedule with the one from the chain config. i'd prefer not to.
